questions.question: An object that moves with constant acceleration:
choices: Must be moving in a circle
questions.explanation: Constant acceleration means the velocity is changing at a constant rate.

questions.question: The equation $d = v_i t + \frac{1}{2}at^2$ is used to calculate:
questions.explanation: This equation calculates displacement (d) when initial velocity (v_i), time (t), and acceleration (a) are known.

questions.question: A car accelerates from rest to 20 m/s in 5 seconds. What is its acceleration?
questions.explanation: Acceleration = change in velocity / time = (20 - 0) / 5 = 4 m/s²

questions.question: A ball is thrown vertically upward with an initial velocity of 15 m/s. How high will it go (assuming g = 10 m/s²)?
questions.explanation: Using v² = v₀² + 2a(x-x₀), at the highest point v = 0, so 0 = 15² + 2(-10)(h), which gives h = 15²/(2×10) = 11.25 m

questions.question: Which of the following is true about free fall motion near Earth's surface (ignoring air resistance)?
choices: All objects fall at the same rate regardless of mass
Heavier objects fall faster than lighter objects
The acceleration decreases as the object falls
The velocity remains constant during the fall
questions.explanation: In the absence of air resistance, all objects fall with the same acceleration regardless of their mass.
